name: Sierra de Villas by Villanueva del Arzobispo — circular by Mogón
name_original: Sierra de Villas por Villanueva del Arzobispo — circular por Mogón
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Santo Tomé traverse a landscape shaped by volcanic origins, featuring dramatic mountains and lush tropical forests. The island offers diverse terrains, from challenging ascents through mountainous regions to serene coastal roads. Cyclists can expect routes winding through historic cocoa and coffee plantations, providing varied backdrops for rides. The topography includes significant elevation changes, making for a rewarding experience for road cyclists. Best road cycling routes around Santo Tomé The most popular road cycling route is Tour of the Cazorla and Las Villas Sierras, a 94.4 miles (151.9 km) trail that takes 8 hours 58 minutes to complete. This difficult route features significant elevation gain through mountainous terrain. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Sierra de Villas by Villanueva del Arzobispo — circular by Mogón, a difficult 65.5 miles (105.4 km) path. This route offers panoramic views as it winds through the Sierra de Villas. Local road cyclists also love the Santo Tomé return via Torreperogil - Mogón and the climb of Osera, a 56.8 miles (91.4 km) trail leading through varied mountainous terrain, often completed in about 4 hours 50 minutes. Road cycling around Santo Tomé is defined by volcanic mountains, lush rainforests, and scenic coastal roads. The network offers options for both moderate and difficult routes, catering to different ability levels. The routes in Santo Tomé are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.75 stars from more than 4 reviews. More than 400 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Santo Tomé's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available in Santo Tomé?
A: There are over 35 road cycling routes around Santo Tomé, offering a diverse range of experiences. These include 15 moderate routes and 21 difficult routes, catering to various skill levels.

Q: What are the typical difficulty levels for road cycling in Santo Tomé?
A: Road cycling in Santo Tomé is characterized by its hilly, volcanic terrain, meaning many routes involve significant elevation changes. You'll find a mix of moderate and difficult routes, with 15 moderate and 21 difficult options available. The challenging ascents are often rewarded with spectacular panoramic views.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes in Santo Tomé?
A: Yes, Santo Tomé offers several circular road cycling routes. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/1344755397/sierra-de-villas-by-villanueva-del-arzobispo-circular-by-mogon" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de Villas by Villanueva del Arzobispo — circular by Mogón&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging 105.4 km path that provides panoramic views as it winds through the Sierra de Villas.

Q: What natural attractions can I see along road cycling routes in Santo Tomé?
A: Road cycling routes in Santo Tomé often pass through stunning natural landscapes. You can encounter lush rainforests, historic cocoa and coffee plantations, and dramatic volcanic formations. The island is also dotted with spectacular waterfalls like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2613975" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salto de los Órganos Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1062504" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Linarejos Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6598554" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Calavera Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;. Some routes also offer views of unique natural monuments such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/183420" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Elías Gorge&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any road cycling routes that offer scenic viewpoints?
A: Absolutely. The volcanic landscape of Santo Tomé provides numerous opportunities for scenic viewpoints. Routes often feature breathtaking views of the ocean, dense forests, and volcanic hills. The effort of climbing is frequently rewarded with stunning vistas, especially along coastal roads like the scenic Santa Catarina Road.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Santo Tomé?
A: While specific seasonal recommendations aren't detailed, Santo Tomé's tropical climate generally offers good cycling conditions year-round. However, it's advisable to consider the island's rainy and dry seasons. The cooler climates in higher altitudes can also provide a more comfortable ride during warmer periods.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about road cycling in Santo Tomé?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.75 stars from more than 4 reviews. Cyclists often praise the unique combination of challenging volcanic terrain, lush tropical ecosystems, and stunning coastal scenery. The opportunity for deep immersion in nature on peaceful roads with minimal traffic is also a significant draw.

Q: Can I find routes that pass through plantations or historical sites?
A: Yes, many road cycling routes wind through historic cocoa and coffee plantations (roças) such as Roça Agostinho Neto and Roça Monte Café. These areas offer a unique cultural and historical backdrop to your ride, allowing you to experience the island's rich heritage while cycling.

Q: Are there any long-distance road cycling routes in Santo Tomé?
A: For those seeking longer distances, Santo Tomé offers several extensive routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1276016024/tour-of-the-cazorla-and-las-villas-sierras" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tour of the Cazorla and Las Villas Sierras&lt;/a&gt;, for example, is a difficult 151.9 km trail with significant elevation gain, typically taking nearly 9 hours to complete.

Q: What kind of natural landscapes will I encounter on the routes?
A: You will encounter a diverse range of natural landscapes, from dense tropical forests within the Ôbo National Park to dramatic volcanic mountains and pristine coastlines. The island's volcanic origins have sculpted a breathtaking topography, including towering peaks like Pico de São Tomé and distinctive formations such as Pico Cão Grande.

Q: Are there any road cycling routes suitable for families?
A: While many routes feature significant elevation changes, the quiet north-west coast is particularly recommended for cycling, with tarmac roads tracing the coastline and passing through pretty beaches and fishing villages. These flatter sections might be more suitable for families or those looking for a less strenuous ride, though specific 'family-friendly' routes are not explicitly detailed in the data.

Q: Where can I find parking for road cycling routes in Santo Tomé?
A: Information on specific parking areas for road cycling routes is not extensively detailed. However, many routes start from towns or villages, where public parking might be available. It's advisable to check the starting points of individual routes for local parking options.
tips: Beautiful road that goes through one of the mountains in the Sierra de Cazorla, with an asphalted road and always with impressive views of the area as a whole!
Small oasis on the banks of the Guadalquivir river, at the foot of the Sierra de las Cuatro Villas. Refreshing river water is captured to provide a beautiful natural pool.

Santo Tomé is located on the slopes of the Sierra de Cazorla, in the valley of the Guadalquivir river. passing the center of the village you can see the bell tower of the stone church, dating from the fourteenth century.
